All right, you're back. Time for the next lesson. Let's roll forward here. Time to get specific. Now in total, there are 29 tonalities that human beings use as they communicate. 29 in total. Now of those 29, 10 of them are referred to in my book as core influencing tonalities, we use them over and over again as we influence and persuade. Now likewise with the straight line system, I've been able to consolidate the infinite number of gestures and postures and facial expressions that comprise so-called body lounge and put them into 10 core principles. Now if you're saying, well, wait a second, I knew there had to be a catch Jordan. You made everything sound so easy. Now I find out I've got to learn 20 different things. There's been 10 tonalities, 10 body lounge principles. How am I supposed to do that? I'm not a kid anymore. I mean, I'm an adult and it's like as an adult, like one of those old dogs, I can't learn new tricks in body language. Those are new tricks Jordan. Now hopefully, that's at least a slight exaggeration of what you're thinking right now. I hope that, right? But if you're feeling even remotely like that, I have one very important thought I'd like to share with you. Ready? Stop it. You don't have to learn anything. Not only do you already know all 10 tonalities and all 10 body language principles, you've used them countless times throughout your entire life, each and every one. The only difference is that in the past, you've been using them unconsciously, meaning without even thinking about it. In other words, there have been countless times in your life when each one of those tonalities came out of you and emanated from your voice naturally as a reaction to what you were actually feeling at the moment. Same thing is true of body language. Let me give you an example. Have you ever had a time in your life when you were feeling just absolutely certain about something? Now if you're having trouble, go back to when you were a teenager, right? You're certain about everything back then. But seriously, you were so certain about something that the tonality of certain just came flying straight out of your mouth. It was if you could literally feel the certainty coming through you, oozing at every one of the pores of your body and anyone who was listening would know there was no doubt that you 100% believed in everything you were saying. Of course you have. We all have. How about telling a secret? How many times in your life have you lowered your voice to just above a whisper to tell someone a secret? Again, we've all done this a thousand times because we intuitively know that a whisper intrigues people and draws them in, compelling them to basically listen more closely. Now in a sales setting, when you apply a whisper precisely the right moment of an oral presentation, you will be shocked at the impact it has on your prospect, especially if you raise your voice right back up afterwards. See, the key here is modulation. You want to lower your voice and then raise your voice. You want to speed up and then slow down. You want to take a declarative statement and turn it into a question. You want to group certain words together and say others in staccato-like beats. Always remember to never stay in any one tonality for too long or else the prospect will become bored or in scientific terms they habituate and tune out. See from my part I am constantly using my tonality in body language to ensure against that. See, tuning out does not happen randomly. It's based on a conscious equation your prospect runs as to whether or not you're a person worth listening to. Prospects ask themselves. Can this person, can this salesperson really help me achieve my goals? Can this salesperson help me get what I want in life? Can they help me resolve the pain I have? Now, if the answer to those questions is a resounding no, guess what? They'll tune out. And if the answer is yeah, they can, well guess what? They will listen. It is as simple as that. That's why it's so absolutely crucial right out of the gate to come off as being sharp as a tack, enthusiastic as hell and an expert in your field. If you do, not only will your prospect hang on your every word, but they'll also, as I said, let you take control of the sale and begin the process of moving them down the straight line from the open to the close and you can do it in the same way every time. Now, here's the deal. In terms of how you actually start using this in the real world, with just a little bit of practice you'll find yourself applying the right tonality and body language unconsciously meaning automatically whenever you find yourself in a situation of influence. But until then, you got to be extra visual in here about this and start consciously applying the right tonality and right body language to every word, every turn of phrase. This will ensure your prospect stays right locked into your magnetic zone and does not tune out. Now, with that, I want to stop for a few moments here. Let's take a quiz, lock this stuff in and then come right back, meet me in the next lesson. I got a special treat for you there. You're going to love it. I'll see you in a few minutes.
